title: The First World War
description: What led to the Great War? How did the United States get involved?
topic_path: The United States in World War I

assessment_items.question_content:
Who were the **Central Powers** in World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: England and France were part of the Triple Entente Alliance, not the Central Powers.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Germany and Austria-Hungary
England and France
Russia and Serbia

assessment_items.question_content:
Who were the **Allies** in World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Germany and Austria-Hungary were part of the Central Powers.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Russia, France, the United Kingdom, and the United States
Germany, Austria-Hungary, the Ottoman Empire, and Bulgaria
The United States, the Soviet Union, and Spain

assessment_items.question_content:
What did the Germans use to sink the **_Lusitania_** (and other ships during World War I)?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Lusitania wasn’t sunk by a warplane.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: U-boats
Floating mines
Fokker Dr. I planes, such as the one famously flown by Manfred von Richthofen, the Red Baron

assessment_items.question_content:
The **_Lusitania_** was a passenger ship traveling across the Atlantic from New York to Liverpool, England. What were the consequences of the Germans sinking the ship?


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The _Lusitania_ was sunk in May, 1915, and the United States wouldn’t enter the war until April 1917.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The United States and Germany engaged in talks and Germany agreed not to attack any more passenger ships.
The United States immediately declared war on Germany after the deaths of over one hundred American citizens.
US Congress immediately passed a resolution banning all American shipping in the Atlantic.

assessment_items.question_content:
The interception of the German secretary of state Arthur Zimmermann’s telegram to Mexico, where he discussed an alliance with Mexico and “ruthless” and unrestricted submarine warfare, was one factor that led to


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The _Titanic_ sank years before Archduke Franz Ferdinand was assassinated.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: US entry into World War I.
the formation of the Maginot Line.
the 1912 sinking of the _Titanic_ by a German U-Boat.

assessment_items.question_content:
Woodrow Wilson’s **Fourteen Points,** which laid out terms for peace after World War I, also sparked the formation of the



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: NATO was formed in the aftermath of the _Second_ World War.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: League of Nations.
United Nations.
North American Treaty Organization (NATO).

assessment_items.question_content:
How were **civil liberties** in the United States curtailed during the First World War?
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: No campaign of unlawful search and seizure was enacted on Canadians or Britons on American soil during this period.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Espionage Act of 1917 and the Sedition Act of 1918 made it a crime to express anti-war sentiment or criticism of the United States Government.
British and Canadian citizens living in the United States were subjected to unlawful searches and seizures.
African-Americans were illegally prohibited from gathering publicly in groups.

assessment_items.question_content:
**President Wilson** responded to the end of World War I by



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There is no such policy as Wilsonian Negation.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: putting forth his Fourteen Points plan, a comprehensive vision for the transformation of world politics.
commencing work on the Manhattan Project.
outlining the Wilsonian Negation, which would set the stage for a generation of American isolationism.

assessment_items.question_content:
How did the **Treaty of Versailles** change the balance of power in Europe?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Germany**, not France or Belgium, was disarmed by the Treaty of Versailles.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It established the League of Nations and explicitly blamed Germany for the outbreak of war, forcing it to pay $\$$5 billion in reparations.
It disarmed France and Belgium, leaving them vulnerable to invasion from Central Europe.
It demanded that Nicholas II abdicate his throne as Emperor of Russia, setting off the Bolshevik Revolution.

assessment_items.question_content:
How did the **Treaty of Versailles** affect the United States’ participation on the world stage?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: _Dove_ and _hawk_ are opposite terms; a dove supports peace and a hawk supports war.  There was no such caucus, and it was the *Republican* Party that took control of Congress in 1918.

**Solution:**
[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Irreconcilables, isolationist members of Congress, prevented the United States from ratifying the treaty; the United States never became a member of the League of Nations.
The United States led the negotiations on the treaty, going on to become one of the leaders of the League of Nations Security Council.
The so-called “Dove-Hawk Caucus” swept in the midterm elections of 1918, giving Democrats control of the Congress.
